description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly grayish-white coloration. The surface is marked by a mix of rough textures, with visible veins and streaks of darker gray and brown, suggesting mineral inclusions or weathering. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings, and its edges are jagged rather than smooth. There are patches of lighter and darker tones, giving the stone a mottled, natural appearance. A small label or tag is affixed to one side, indicating it may have been cataloged or collected for study. The overall look is that of a naturally occurring rock, possibly used as an obstacle or barrier in an urban or landscaped setting.
description: A rugged, irregular grayish-white stone with mottled veining and rough, jagged surfaces. It features natural mineral streaks and a catalog label on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows a broad, uneven face with prominent veining and a visible catalog label near the bottom edge.
description_view_1: The stone appears elongated and more angular from this side, with a pronounced ridge and less visible labeling.
description_view_2: This angle highlights the stone's irregular, blocky shape and rough surface texture, with subtle color variations.
description_view_3: A wide, flat face is visible here, showing extensive mottling and veining, as well as the catalog label on the lower left.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular veining and mottling with streaks of gray and brown, no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible mineral streaks and some surface discoloration indicating exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured surfaces, but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements nearby.
